items.title: Goldman Pitches Investors on Loans to Their Own Private Funds
items.description: For years, Goldman Sachs Group Inc. has provided key financing to private markets funds that bridges the gap between when they receive investor commitments and when they actually deploy that money.

items.title: Calpers Notches 14.8% Return in Fiscal 2026, Driven By Stocks
items.description: The California Public Employees’ Retirement System reported a 14.8% gain for its latest fiscal year, with results driven by stocks and private equity.

items.title: Waller Says Fed May Need to Raise Rates to Tame Core Inflation
items.description: Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller said policymakers may need to raise rates in the near term if underlying inflation continues to signal broad price pressures.

items.title: Iran Shipped 57 Million Barrels of Crude Between US Blockades
items.description: Iran managed to export at least 57 million barrels of crude during a brief gap between two American naval blockades, highlighting what’s at stake for the global oil market now that restrictions are be

items.title: First Public Fusion Company Surges in Wall Street Trading Debut
items.description: General Fusion Group Ltd. surged in its first day of trading after merging with a blank-check firm, a sign of investors’ interest in the world’s first publicly listed nuclear fusion company.

items.title: Financing the AI Buildout is About to Get Trickier
items.description: Hyperscalers will need to get more creative.

items.title: Russia’s Oil Output Falls to Lowest in at Least 2 1/2 Years
items.description: Russia’s crude output declined to the lowest in at least two and a half years in June as Ukraine attacked the nation’s oil infrastructure on an almost daily basis.

items.title: Europe Risks ‘Explosive’ Path If It Doesn’t Fix Debts, IMF Warns
items.description: Europe’s sovereign-debt dynamics are in danger of going badly awry if the region doesn’t get a grip on its public finances, the International Monetary Fund said.

items.title: Connecticut Towns Face Water-Fee Hikes as Bonds Finance Buyout
items.description: Dozens of towns in the state known for its hedge funds and Wall Street commuters are about to feel the impact of a rare type of leveraged buyout.

items.title: UBS Sees Goldman, JPMorgan in Focus on Busy Earnings Day
items.description: UBS Group AG analyst Erika Najarian says Goldman Sachs Group Inc. may have the tallest order when it comes to demonstrating earnings prowess this week, while investors may be focused on more on succes

items.title: Top Risks Facing Bank Earnings
items.description: Big bank earnings kick off with expectations running sky-high. KBW's Chris McGratty joins Open Interest to explain why capital markets remain a major tailwind, why deposit costs and credit quality cou

items.title: Zurich Ranks Top in Disposable Income Poll Despite $7 Coffees
items.description: Zurich and Geneva may be the most expensive places in the world to buy your meat and veg but it’ll still pay off as the two Swiss cities top a new survey of the highest disposable incomes in the world

items.title: Apollo’s Slok Warns of Dollar’s Vulnerability to an AI Pullback
items.description: The dollar’s recent gains are at risk if the selloff in artificial intelligence stocks deepens, according to Apollo Global Management Inc.’s Torsten Slok.

items.title: American Aid Worker With Ebola ‘Stable’ in German Hospital
items.description: An American aid worker who tested positive for Ebola is stable and being treated in a specialized isolation unit at Frankfurt University Hospital in Germany, according to a statement from the hospital

items.description: The biggest US banks — set to kick off a marathon Tuesday with five of those firms reporting second-quarter earnings — are reaping the benefits of a volatile past few months that’s spurred

items.title: Bloomberg Surveillance 7/13/2026
items.description: Jonathan Ferro, Lisa Abramowicz and Annmarie Hordern speak daily with leaders and decision makers from Wall Street to Washington and beyond. No other program better positions investors and executives

items.title: Tech Stocks Slide as Oil Climbs on Hormuz Standoff
items.description: A selloff in tech giants sent stocks lower, with the market also falling as oil jumped after clashes over the Strait of Hormuz raised concerns about a disruption in energy supplies that could fuel inf

items.title: Trump Says US Will Be Reimbursed 20% Rate for Hormuz Traffic
items.description: President Donald Trump said the US would reinstate its blockade of Iranian ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz and demanded a 20% reimbursement on all other cargo shipped through the waterway.

items.title: Cocoa’s Uneven Demand Recovery Meets Fresh Price Volatility
items.description: Cocoa demand is expected to remain uneven across the world, suggesting the recovery remains fragile as renewed supply concerns push bean prices higher and threaten to keep chocolate pricier for longer

items.title: Disney Exiting Streaming Could Spur 40% Rally: Wells Fargo
items.description: Walt Disney Co. shares have been in a slump for years, but Wells Fargo Securities said there’s one possible move that could reverse the trend: ditching its streaming-video business.
